title: Election Day Act
plain_summary: The Election Day Act would make Election Day a new federal holiday. This means that federal government workers would get the day off from work on Election Day. The goal is to make it easier for people to vote by giving them time off. Right now, Election Day is not an official federal holiday like Christmas or Independence Day. If this bill passes, it would be added to the list of days when federal employees do not have to work.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Oversight and Government Reform.

sections.title: Section 1  Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Election Day Act.

sections.title: Section 2  Patriot Day
sections.text: This section changes the federal law about government holidays. It adds Election Day to the official list of federal holidays. Election Day will be listed right after Columbus Day in the law that governs when federal employees get days off work.
